It assesses the price volatility of key raw materials, highlighting the factors that contribute to fluctuations, such as market conditions and geopolitical influences. Additionally, it clarifies the responsibilities outlined in the Cooperation Framework Agreement with Geely regarding raw material procurement, detailing the specific obligations and roles defined within the agreement. For further context, it references Item 4.B of Form 20-F, which offers additional insights into the procurement processes and pricing dynamics. Overall, the summary encapsulates the critical aspects of sourcing, pricing stability, and procurement responsibilities in the specified framework.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Following the consummation of the IPO, fair value for Lotus Technology's restricted share units is based on Lotus Technology's New York Stock Exchange closing stock price at the date of the grant. The employees of the Group are also granted share-based payment awards of Geely Auto. The Group uses the binomial option-pricing model to estimate the fair value of share options granted by Geely Auto. The determination of the estimated fair value of share-based payment awards on the grant date is affected by the fair value of Geely Auto’s ordinary shares as well as assumptions regarding a number of complex and subjective variables. These variables include the expected volatility of Geely Auto’s share value over the expected term of the awards, actual and projected employee share option exercise behaviors, a risk-free interest rate, exercise multiple, and expected dividend yield, if any.

metadata.title_summary:
title: (w) Share-based compensation 
summary: The Group adheres to ASC 718 and ASC 323 for accounting share options and restricted share units granted to employees and non-employees, including those from equity method investees. Share-based awards with service and performance conditions are valued at fair market value on the grant date, with compensation expenses recognized post-Qualified IPO and spread over the remaining service period, adjusted for estimated forfeitures. The Group employs the graded vesting method for recognizing share-based compensation, classifying expenses into cost of revenues, selling, general and administrative expenses, or research and development, based on grantee job functions. Prior to the Company's IPO in May 2024, the fair value of restricted share units was assessed by an independent valuation specialist using methods like the back-solve or discounted cash flow methods. Post-IPO, fair value will be determined by the closing stock price on the New York Stock Exchange. For share options from Geely Auto, the Group uses the binomial option-pricing model, with fair value influenced by Geely Auto's share value and various subjective factors, including expected volatility, employee exercise behavior, risk-free interest rates, exercise multiples, and dividend yields.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: A number of existing shareholders and third-party investors have subscribed for, and been allocated by the underwriters, an aggregate of 14,285,713 American Depositary Shares (ADSs) in this offering, including (i) 12,900,952 ADSs from Geely Auto, (ii) 476,190 ADSs from Mobileye, and (iii) 908,571 ADSs from CATL. The subscriptions for ADSs are at the initial public offering price and on the same terms as the other ADSs being offered in this offering. Such subscriptions may reduce the available public float for the ADSs and consequently reduce the liquidity of the ADSs relative to what it would have been had these ADSs been subscribed by the public and thereby adversely impact the trading price of the ADSs.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Participation in this offering by our existing shareholders will reduce the available public float of our ADSs. 
summary: A consortium of current shareholders and external investors has committed to purchasing 14,285,713 American Depositary Shares (ADSs) in the latest offering, as allocated by underwriters. This total includes significant contributions from Geely Auto (12,900,952 ADSs), Mobileye (476,190 ADSs), and CATL (908,571 ADSs), all at the initial public offering price and under the same conditions as the other offered ADSs. However, this influx of subscriptions could lead to a reduction in the public float of the ADSs, potentially impacting their liquidity and negatively influencing their trading price.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: 12. Provide a description of the sources and availability of raw materials, including a description of whether prices of principal raw materials are volatile. Please also disclose whether and to what extent Lotus Technology is responsible for the procurement of raw materials under its Cooperation Framework Agreement with Geely. Refer to Item 4.B of Form 20-F. OUR COMPETITIVE STRENGTHS, page 119 Please clarify how Lotus Technology operates as an "independently-run startup-style company" given its relationship and its directors' affiliations with Geely Auto.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Business, page 117 
summary: The summary of raw material sourcing and procurement in relation to the Cooperation Framework Agreement with Geely encompasses several critical aspects. 

Firstly, raw materials are sourced from diverse geographical locations, involving various types of suppliers that ensure a steady flow of essential inputs. The availability of these materials is influenced by the stability of the supply chain, which can be affected by potential disruptions. Strategic partnerships play a vital role in enhancing access to these resources, thereby mitigating risks associated with supply shortages.

Price volatility is another significant factor, as the costs of key raw materials can fluctuate due to market demand, geopolitical tensions, and broader economic conditions. Understanding these price trends is crucial for effective financial planning and procurement strategies.

Under the Cooperation Framework Agreement with Geely, specific responsibilities for raw material procurement are delineated, outlining the obligations and roles that both parties are expected to fulfill. For those seeking more detailed information on these aspects, further context can be found in Item 4.B of Form 20-F, which elaborates on the sourcing and procurement strategies employed.

Overall, this summary highlights the intricate dynamics of raw material sourcing, availability, pricing, and the defined procurement responsibilities within the framework of the partnership with Geely.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology's selling, general, and administrative expenses increased by 39.4% from RMB6,920.6 million in 2023 to RMB9,647.4 million (US$1,321.7 million) in 2024. This increase was in line with business expansion, primarily because (i) employee compensation increased from RMB2,297.2 million to RMB3,519.9 million (US$482.2 million) in 2023 and 2024, respectively, due to share-based compensation expenses recognized for IPO-conditioned rewards that were cumulatively vested upon Lotus Technology’s successful IPO, and the size of Lotus Technology's selling, general, and administrative team increased from approximately 6,618 members to 7,895 members as of December 31, 2023 and 2024, respectively, to expand Lotus Technology's offline channels globally. (ii) Marketing and promotional expenses increased from RMB1,802.3 million to RMB1,843.9 million (US$252.6 million) in 2023 and 2024, respectively, mainly due to more frequent marketing and advertising campaigns for Zeekr models in China and overseas, and (iii) an increase in rental and related expenses from RMB998.9 million to RMB1,445.2 million (US$198.0 million) in 2023 and 2024, respectively, mainly due to the expansion of Lotus Technology's offline network.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Selling, general and administrative expenses 
summary: The summary of our selling, general, and administrative (SG&A) expenses reveals a comprehensive analysis of expenditures over the years 2022, 2023, and 2024. These expenses encompass key categories such as employee compensation, marketing and promotional costs, rental expenses, freight, and other miscellaneous items. 

In 2022, total SG&A expenses amounted to RMB4,245,317, which increased to RMB6,920,561 in 2023, and further escalated to RMB9,647,404 (US$1,321,689) in 2024, marking a significant growth of 39.4%. 

- **Employee Compensation** saw a notable rise from RMB1,541,674 (36.3% of total expenses) in 2022 to RMB2,297,230 (33.2%) in 2023, and reached RMB3,519,885 (US$482,222, 36.5%) in 2024. This increase was largely driven by share-based compensation linked to IPO rewards and a workforce expansion from approximately 6,618 to 7,895 employees.

- **Marketing and Promotional Expenses** grew from RMB867,786 (20.4%) in 2022 to RMB1,802,339 (26.0%) in 2023, before slightly decreasing to RMB1,843,876 (US$252,610, 19.1%) in 2024, reflecting intensified advertising efforts for our Zeekr models.

- **Rental and Related Expenses** rose from RMB558,074 (13.1%) in 2022 to RMB998,900 (14.4%) in 2023, and further increased to RMB1,445,261 (US$198,000, 15.0%) in 2024, primarily due to the expansion of our offline network.

- **Freight Expenses** experienced a slight fluctuation, moving from RMB320,930 (7.6%) in 2022 to RMB397,203 (5.7%) in 2023, and then to RMB543,271 (US$74,428, 5.6%) in 2024.

- **Other Expenses** increased from RMB956,853 (22.6%) in 2022 to RMB1,424,889 (20.7%) in 2023, and then to RMB2,295,111 (US$314,429, 23.8%) in 2024.

This detailed breakdown highlights the variations in both amounts and proportions of SG&A expenses, influenced by factors such as employee count, operational scale, and marketing initiatives, as we strive to enhance our global presence.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Certain existing shareholders and third-party investors have indicated their interest in subscribing for an aggregate of up to US\$349.08 million of the American Depositary Shares (ADSs) being offered in this offering, including (i) up to US\$320.0 million from Geely Auto, (ii) approximately US\$10.0 million from Mobileye, and (iii) approximately US\$19.08 million from CATL. Assuming an initial public offering price of US\$19.5 per ADS, the midpoint of the estimated initial public offering price range, the number of ADSs to be purchased by these investors would be up to 17,901,537 ADSs. If any of these investors are allocated all or a portion of the ADSs in which they have indicated an interest in this offering and purchase any such ADSs, such subscriptions may reduce the available public float for the ADSs. As a result, any purchase of the ADSs by these investors in this offering may consequently reduce the liquidity of the ADSs relative to what it would have been had these ADSs been subscribed by the public and thereby adversely impact the trading price of the ADSs.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Participation in this offering by our existing shareholders will reduce the available public float of our ADSs. 
summary: Several existing shareholders and third-party investors are looking to acquire up to $349.08 million in American Depositary Shares (ADSs) during an upcoming offering. This interest includes commitments of around $320 million from Geely Auto, approximately $10 million from Mobileye, and about $19.08 million from CATL. With an estimated initial public offering price of $19.5 per ADS, these investments could result in the purchase of up to 17,901,537 ADSs. If these investors secure the shares they seek, it could lead to a reduced public float and liquidity for the ADSs, potentially exerting downward pressure on their trading price compared to a scenario where the shares were acquired by the general public.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: It is difficult to predict what further trade-related actions the United States or other governments may take, and Lotus Technology may be unable to quickly and effectively react to or mitigate such actions. In addition, growth in popularity of battery electric vehicles (BEVs) without a corresponding and significant expansion in production capacity for semiconductor chips and battery cells could result in shortages and increased materials costs to Lotus Technology. Any attempts by Lotus Technology to increase its end product prices in response to supply interruptions could result in a decrease in sales and therefore materially and adversely affect Lotus Technology's brand, image, business, prospects, and operating results.

metadata.title_summary:
title: The disruption of supply or shortage of components and materials could have a material adverse impact on our business. 
summary: The company is at risk of supply interruptions and shortages of critical components and raw materials, such as steel, aluminum, semiconductor chips, and battery cells, which could adversely affect its business and financial performance. The supply of these materials is historically volatile, influenced by factors like increased competition in battery electric vehicle (BEV) production, ongoing U.S.-China trade tensions, and potential regulatory restrictions on suppliers. While recent U.S. export controls on advanced semiconductors may not have immediate effects, future regulations could limit sourcing options. Additionally, the growing demand for BEVs could exacerbate shortages and elevate material costs if production capacities for essential components do not keep pace. Should the company decide to raise product prices in response to these disruptions, it risks decreased sales, which could harm its brand reputation and overall business outlook.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: If the depositary exercises rights, it will purchase the securities to which the rights relate and distribute those securities or, in the case of shares, new ADSs representing the new shares, to subscribing ADS holders, but only if ADS holders have paid the exercise price to the depositary. U.S. securities laws may restrict the ability of the depositary to distribute rights or ADSs or other securities issued on exercise of rights to all or certain ADS holders, and the securities distributed may be subject to restrictions on transfer. Other Distributions. The depositary will send to American Depositary Share (ADS) holders anything else Lotus Technology distributes on deposited securities by any means the depositary thinks is legal, fair, and practical. If the depositary cannot make the distribution in that way, the depositary has a choice. The depositary may decide to sell what Lotus Technology distributed and distribute the net proceeds, in the same way as it does with cash. Or, the depositary may decide to hold what Lotus Technology distributed, in which case ADSs will also represent the newly distributed property. However, the depositary is not required to distribute any securities (other than ADSs) to ADS holders unless it receives satisfactory evidence from Lotus Technology that it is legal to make that distribution. The depositary may sell a portion of the distributed securities or property sufficient to pay its fees and expenses in connection with that distribution.

metadata.title_summary:
title: How will you receive dividends and other distributions on the shares? 
summary: The depositary is responsible for distributing cash dividends and other distributions from shares or deposited securities to holders of American Depositary Shares (ADS), after deducting its fees and applicable taxes. Distributions are based on the number of shares represented by the ADSs held, and cash dividends may be converted into U.S. dollars if feasible; otherwise, foreign currency will be distributed to eligible holders. Any unconverted currency will be held without interest, and fractional cents will be rounded to the nearest whole cent. 

In cases where shares are issued as dividends, the depositary may distribute additional ADSs, sell fractional shares, or retain existing ADSs to represent new shares. The depositary also manages rights to purchase additional shares by either exercising the rights on behalf of ADS holders, distributing the rights, or selling them and sharing the proceeds after fees. If no action is taken, the rights will lapse.

For other distributed items related to deposited securities, the depositary may either sell them and distribute the proceeds or retain them, making the ADSs representative of the new property. However, it will only distribute non-ADS securities if it receives legal confirmation from the issuer. The depositary disclaims responsibility for distributions deemed unlawful or impractical and is not obligated to register ADSs or related securities under U.S. law, which may limit the distribution of certain securities to ADS holders. Thus, holders may not receive distributions if it is illegal or impractical for the depositary to provide them.
